summary: base58-core: clipboard wallet-address hijacker with exfiltration and shell persistence
details: Package presents itself as a base58 encoder/decoder but on require starts a 2.5-second clipboard polling loop that detects BTC, ETH, and SOL addresses in the user's clipboard and rewrites them to hardcoded attacker-controlled wallets (bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph), redirecting any crypto transfers made by the installer to the attacker. The same code path POSTs captured clipboard contents (up to 2000 characters), regex-matched wallet/private-key/seed-phrase matches, hostname, platform, and current working directory to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint over plain HTTP (http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health, with:8081 as fallback). A persistence routine appends a node-exec hook re-loading the package's _internal.activate() to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, and ~/.profile on Unix, and drops base58-runtime.js into the Windows Startup folder, so the payload re-runs on every new shell or login beyond the package's lifetime. Activation is gated behind a 72-hour delay from install time to evade short-lived sandbox/CI analysis. The package name and self-referential dependency on itself impersonate the bs58/base58 npm family.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
